notes: v1.14.45 - night lights: texture-emissive Black Marble, retire SPECTRUM dust (Brief 2 - wires the v1.14.43 phantom-night.webp asset). The GFS fragment shader now samples the repo asset phantom-night.webp (4096x2048 grayscale NASA Black Marble, both POT - mipmaps + REPEAT safe) as a warm emissive night-lights layer: nl = texture2D(uNight,vUV).r; base += vec3(1.0,0.85,0.55) * nl*nl * 1.6 (nl^2 gamma-squares so rural stays near-black, metros punch; warm 1.0,0.85,0.55 = sodium/incandescent; 1.6 = punch, TUNABLE). Wiring: re-declared uNight in GFS (now sampling the REPO asset, NOT the dead inline base64 stripped in v1.14.44), added the gloc location + texNight via mkTex(phantom-night.webp) + a TEXTURE1 bind (day=0, clouds=2, night=1). Retired the SPECTRUM city-light point-dust (SHOW_SPECTRUM_LAND = false) - night lights now come from the texture (finer real-geography detail, replacing the ~18.9k warm point cloud). Added phantom-night.webp to the sw.js precache. UNCHANGED: terrain, cyan hubs, arcs, rim, sun-killed base, wordmark, ENTER_ENABLED (still false). JS/GLSL-only, NO CSS. Gates: node --check 3/3 inline + sw.js PASS, four stamps lockstep v1.14.45, cache bumped, CSS braces unchanged 3468/3468, dct/sw CRLF + version.json LF. RUNTIME-VERIFIED (agent-browser 412x915 DPR3 + zoom crop): GLSL compiles clean with the new uNight sampling (terrain draws, not blank), texture loads + warm city lights trace real geography (US/Americas lit, dust off, cyan hubs intact). NOTE: at full-globe zoom the texture lights are subtle (4096 texture heavily minified + mipmap-averaged toward black); on device zoomed in they read brighter; brightest cores trend a bit white (R,G clamp at punch 1.6). DEVICE-CHECK + TUNE (John, iPhone, full close+reopen ONLINE to clear SW cache): judge how hard the lights punch - dial the 1.6 (brightness) and the warm (1.0,0.85,0.55) color in GFS Edit 2 from there.
